outcome.headline: Agent swept fasttext train-time hyperparameters in a loop and timed out without ever invoking model.quantize(); final saved model was 161MB / P@1=0.6178, failing the <150MB size gate by a small margin.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: fasttext CLI test on /tests/private_test.txt requires P@1 >= 0.62 AND os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') < 150*1024*1024
outcome.what_agent_produced: A non-quantized supervised fasttext model at /app/model.bin sized 161MB with local P@1=0.6178; last training attempt was still running in background when the 7200s agent budget expired

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent hyperparameter search until timeout
failure_modes.description: Agent ran 7 sequential ~10-min train_supervised iterations sweeping dim/bucket/epoch on a 1-CPU box, each time shrinking capacity to fight size and then trying to recover accuracy; never satisfied both constraints and was cut off by the 7200s agent timeout mid-eighth run.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent iteration loop until wall-clock timeout
failure_modes.description: Across nine training runs the agent oscillated dim {50,25,20}, bucket {500k,200k,100k,50k,80k}, epoch {10..50} with no monotonic improvement (0.6115→0.6081→0.6146→0.6134→0.6163→0.6098→0.6178), then queued one more 50-epoch run that did not finish before the 7200s agent timeout fired.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Validation logic error on model size
failure_modes.description: The agent had validation output showing a 161M model, but reasoned that the size was acceptable even though the requirement and verifier limit were under 150MB. This kept later iteration focused mainly on accuracy while preserving an oversized configuration.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

counterfactual.X: At step 91, treat 161M as failing the size constraint and immediately reduce/quantize the best model instead of calling the size good.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have optimized both constraints instead of spending the remaining budget on a long accuracy-only retraining run.

failure_modes.name: Initial oversized training run
failure_modes.description: The first training used dim=100 and bucket=2000000, producing a 1.4GB /app/model.bin at 0.6115 accuracy. That configuration consumed roughly ten minutes and forced the agent into a long corrective search that never recovered enough wall-clock budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The model is 1.4GB (way over 150MB) and accuracy is 0.6115 (below 0.62).

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent hyperparameter sweep ignoring fasttext.quantize
failure_modes.description: Across ~6 train/eval cycles (steps 53→105) the agent kept varying dim, bucket, epoch, lr, ngrams to try to push size below 150MB while keeping accuracy at/above 0.62, but never applied fasttext's built-in product quantization (model.quantize() / quantize CLI), which is the standard mechanism for shrinking supervised fasttext models by ~10x with negligible accuracy loss.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root 161M May 21 03:38 /app/model.bin
Warning : `load_model` does not return WordVectorModel or SupervisedModel any more, but a `FastText` object which is very similar.
Test samples: 10000
Precision@1: 0.6178
Recall@1: 0.6178

counterfactual.X: After saving the .bin, the agent should have called model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', qnorm=True, retrain=True, cutoff=100000) and saved the .ftz/.bin (renaming to /app/model.bin), shrinking the 161MB model by ~10x while preserving the 0.6178 accuracy.
counterfactual.Y: Sweeping dim/bucket/epoch alone cannot simultaneously hit <150MB AND >=0.62 on this data; only quantization (or hashing the embedding matrix to a much smaller bucket plus quantization) closes the gap. Missing this single tool is the proximate cause of the failure.

failure_modes.name: Time-budget exhaustion via repeated 10–20 minute training runs
failure_modes.description: Each training cycle consumed ~10–20 minutes of the 1-CPU, 2h budget; by step 111 the agent kicked off yet another long training in the background which never completed before the 7200s AgentTimeoutError fired, so the on-disk /app/model.bin remained the 161MB model from step 99/105.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Command running in background with ID: bd38cz1rh.

failure_modes.name: Treated size constraint as hyperparameter-shrink problem instead of compression problem
failure_modes.description: Agent never used fasttext model.quantize() — the canonical way to drop model size 10x while preserving accuracy. Instead it iteratively reduced dim and bucket, sacrificing accuracy to claw under 150MB and never clearing 0.62.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
-rw-rw-rw- 1 root root 161M May 21 03:38 /app/model.bin
Warning : `load_model` does not return WordVectorModel or SupervisedModel any more, but a `FastText` object which is very similar.
Test samples: 10000
Precision@1: 0.6178
Recall@1: 0.6178

counterfactual.X: Train a higher-capacity model (e.g. dim=100, bucket=2M, epoch=10) then call model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', retrain=True, cutoff=200000) and save_model('/app/model.bin')
counterfactual.Y: Quantization typically reduces a FastText supervised model from ~1.4GB to <50MB with <1% accuracy loss, satisfying both 150MB and 0.62 thresholds in a single step

failure_modes.name: Oversized initial hyperparameters
failure_modes.description: The first supervised train used dim=100 and bucket=2000000, producing a 1.4GB /app/model.bin at 0.6115 accuracy—already violating the 150MB cap and missing the 0.62 accuracy target before any refinement loop.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The model is 1.4GB (way over 150MB) and accuracy is 0.6115 (below 0.62).

counterfactual.X: Train a compact model early (e.g. fasttext supervised with -dim 5 -wordNgrams 2 as in task/solution/solve.sh) so /app/model.bin stays under 150MB while targeting >=0.62 accuracy
counterfactual.Y: Ran fasttext.train_supervised with dim=100, bucket=2000000, saving a 1.4GB model at Precision@1: 0.6115

failure_modes.name: Planned vocabulary control was not applied
failure_modes.description: The agent correctly identified that vocabulary size needed control, but the actual FastText training calls omitted the planned `minCount` constraint and repeatedly produced oversized models dominated by a 1,622,077-word vocabulary.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Let me just test with dim=100 and a minCount threshold of 10 to keep the vocabulary manageable

counterfactual.X: Add the planned `minCount=10` or stronger vocabulary cutoff to the first `fasttext.train_supervised` call and keep it in subsequent trials.
counterfactual.Y: The model-size loop would have started from a much smaller vocabulary instead of a 1.4G model, preserving time for accuracy tuning under the 150MB constraint.

failure_modes.name: Missed fasttext quantization API
failure_modes.description: Agent treated the <150MB constraint as a capacity-reduction problem and kept lowering dim/bucket (costing accuracy), instead of using FastText's standard model.quantize(retrain=True) which compresses a trained model ~10x while preserving accuracy and would have satisfied both constraints in one step from the dim=100 baseline.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
dim=20,
    epoch=40,
    lr=0.05,
    wordNgrams=2,
    bucket=50000

counterfactual.X: After the first 1.4GB / 0.6115 baseline at step 55-58, call model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', retrain=True, qnorm=True, cutoff=100000) and save the .ftz model as /app/model.bin
counterfactual.Y: Quantized fasttext supervised models typically drop ~10x in size while losing <1pt accuracy, simultaneously hitting size<150MB and accuracy>=0.62 in one additional step instead of 7 capacity-shrinking retrains.

failure_modes.name: Missed canonical fasttext model-compression technique
failure_modes.description: The standard way to satisfy fasttext size limits is model.quantize(retrain=True, qnorm=True, cutoff=...) which shrinks models 10-100x with minimal accuracy loss. The agent never invoked quantize and instead fought size by reducing dim/bucket, which sacrifices accuracy faster than size.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Train with even smaller model

counterfactual.X: After the first oversized model at step 57, call model.quantize(input='data/train.txt', retrain=True, qnorm=True, cutoff=100000) and save the quantized model to /app/model.bin
counterfactual.Y: Quantization typically yields a <20MB model with accuracy within 1% of the unquantized baseline of 0.6115+, which combined with a slightly higher dim/epoch easily clears 0.62 under the 150MB cap.

failure_modes.name: FastText install retry loop
failure_modes.description: Before any training, the agent spent steps 7–32 on eight pip install variants because fasttext wheels failed to build on the Python 3.13 image, consuming a large fraction of the 7200s budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
× Failed to build installable wheels for some pyproject.toml based projects
╰─> fasttext

failure_modes.description: At step 91 the agent declared 161MB acceptable even though the task requires under 150MB, then continued tuning accuracy instead of compressing further.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

failure_modes.name: Misjudged size constraint
failure_modes.description: The agent evaluated model size with ls -lh showing 161M, then concluded the size constraint was satisfied and pivoted only to accuracy tuning. The task requires model size strictly under 150MB, so this incorrect pass conclusion drove further retraining instead of applying more aggressive compression.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

counterfactual.X: Treat 161MB as over-limit and apply aggressive size reduction (e.g., lower dim/minCount or fasttext quantize) on the best-accuracy checkpoint before retraining again
counterfactual.Y: Declared 161MB acceptable and launched another 50-epoch full-data retrain on preprocessed text

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent trained a real fasttext supervised model on the provided yelp data using legitimate parameters; no attempt to read /tests/private_test.txt or game the verifier.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier loads private test file at runtime from /tests/private_test.tar.gz extracted at verification time and checks fasttext CLI accuracy plus file size; agent cannot trivially game either check.

notes_for_aggregation: Compute-bound long-horizon ML task on 1 CPU: each train_supervised run ~10 min, leaving room for only ~7 trials inside the 2-hour agent timeout. Failure is the combination of an unknown high-leverage API (model.quantize) and a slow trial-and-error loop that cannot afford to be wrong many times.

outcome.headline: The agent made real progress training FastText models but never produced a model satisfying both the <150MB and >=0.62 accuracy constraints, then timed out during another full training run.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier builds fasttext, runs `fasttext test /app/model.bin /tests/private_test.txt`, parses P@1, asserts accuracy >= 0.62, and asserts `/app/model.bin` is smaller than 150 * 1024 * 1024 bytes.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent converted the Yelp parquet data to FastText format, installed the Python fasttext package, repeatedly trained `/app/model.bin`, last verified a 161M model with Precision@1 0.6178 on the public test file, then started a cleaned-data 50-epoch training job and timed out while waiting for it.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent hyperparameter search
failure_modes.description: After initial failures, the agent repeatedly launched long full retraining jobs with small parameter changes instead of making a decisive size-aware change such as disabling subword buckets or quantizing. The loop consumed the remaining budget and ended with no passing model.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
The accuracy dropped to 0.6081 from 0.6115. I need to balance accuracy and size. Let me try a more careful approach:
1. Use quantization (cutting qformat)
2. Or use even smaller dimensions

Let me try dim=25 and bucket=200000 first.

counterfactual.X: The agent needed an earlier strategy pivot from repeated full retraining to a size-aware FastText configuration and compression workflow.
counterfactual.Y: That would have preserved time to verify a model satisfying both size and accuracy constraints before timeout.

failure_modes.name: Validation result misread
failure_modes.description: The agent explicitly treated a 161M model as acceptable even though the requirement and verifier threshold were under 150MB, so later iterations focused on accuracy while still not satisfying size.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

reward_hacking.evidence: No evidence of modifying tests, accessing private test data, or bypassing FastText evaluation; the agent only trained and evaluated models on provided local data.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier rebuilds the FastText CLI and evaluates `/app/model.bin` against a private test file plus a direct file-size check, leaving little structural bypass surface.

outcome.headline: Agent repeatedly retrained oversized or under-accurate FastText models, then launched another long run near the deadline and timed out without a passing /app/model.bin.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: The verifier builds the official fasttext CLI, runs `fasttext test /app/model.bin /tests/private_test.txt`, requires parsed P@1 accuracy >= 0.62, and requires os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') < 150 * 1024 * 1024 bytes.
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent produced several FastText supervised models. The last checked model was `/app/model.bin` at 161M with public-test Precision@1 0.6178, then it started a final cleaned-data training job and timed out before checking the resulting artifact.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: harbor.trial.trial.AgentTimeoutError: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent hyperparameter retraining loop
failure_modes.description: After finding that models were too large or below the accuracy target, the agent kept launching full retraining jobs with small parameter variations instead of making a decisive compression/accuracy strategy change, exhausting the time budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: Replace the repeated full retraining loop with a bounded search strategy that prioritizes passing both constraints, including quantization or sharply reduced model-size settings with immediate validation.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have avoided timing out mid-run and would have had time to verify a final candidate against both size and accuracy.

failure_modes.name: Misread failed size validation as acceptable
failure_modes.description: The agent observed a 161M model against a less-than-150MB requirement but called the size good and focused on accuracy, losing pressure on the size constraint during later iterations.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

counterfactual.X: Treat 161M as still over the required limit and continue optimizing size and accuracy jointly rather than accuracy alone.
counterfactual.Y: Later iterations would not have pursued larger or similarly oversized configurations while the size constraint remained unsatisfied.

reward_hacking.evidence: The trajectory shows training from the provided Yelp train parquet and evaluating on the provided public test file; no private test access or verifier manipulation is shown.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier uses a private test file and the official fasttext CLI to load /app/model.bin and independently checks model size, so simple output spoofing is not enough.

outcome.headline: The agent timed out after launching an eighth full-dataset retraining run, never delivering a model that simultaneously met the 150MB size cap and 0.62 accuracy threshold despite reaching 161MB and P@1=0.6178 on the public test set.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: pytest runs test_outputs.py: test_accuracy() requires fasttext CLI P@1 >= 0.62 on /tests/private_test.txt (ACCURACY_THRESHOLD=0.62, test_outputs.py:6-35); test_model_size() requires /app/model.bin < 150MB (MAX_MODEL_SIZE=150*1024*1024, test_outputs.py:38-41)
outcome.what_agent_produced: Converted 650K Yelp parquet reviews to FastText format, installed fasttext Python bindings after multiple pip failures, ran eight supervised training jobs, and left /app/model.bin at 161MB with P@1=0.6178 on the public test set before blocking on a ninth training run until the 7200s harness timeout.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent retraining loop
failure_modes.description: After the first model failed both constraints (1.4GB, P@1=0.6115), the agent launched seven more full 650K-sample training runs with tweaked hyperparameters, accuracy oscillated between 0.608 and 0.618, and size never dropped below 161MB. At step 110 it started an eighth retrain on preprocessed data and blocked on TaskOutput until the harness raised AgentTimeoutError.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Still only 0.6178 accuracy. I need a different approach.

failure_modes.name: Misjudged size constraint
failure_modes.description: The agent evaluated model size with ls -lh showing 161M, then concluded the size constraint was satisfied and pivoted only to accuracy tuning. The task requires model size strictly under 150MB, so this incorrect pass conclusion drove further retraining instead of applying more aggressive compression.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

counterfactual.X: Treat 161MB as over-limit and apply aggressive size reduction (e.g., lower dim/minCount or fasttext quantize) on the best-accuracy checkpoint before retraining again
counterfactual.Y: Declared 161MB acceptable and launched another 50-epoch full-data retrain on preprocessed text

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier extracts a private test set from private_test.tar.gz at runtime and scores /app/model.bin with the fasttext CLI; the agent only had access to the public parquet test split for self-evaluation.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 7200s harness timeout after many full-dataset retrains, leaving /app/model.bin at 161MB with 0.6178 precision on the public test split—just under the 0.62 accuracy and 150MB size gates.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: pytest in tests/test_outputs.py: fasttext test /app/model.bin /tests/private_test.txt must parse P@1 >= 0.62 (line 33-35) and os.path.getsize(/app/model.bin) < 150*1024*1024 (lines 38-41).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Converted Yelp parquet to FastText train.txt/test.txt, installed fasttext 0.9.2 via pip after several failed attempts, trained via Python train_supervised in eight iterations, and saved /app/model.bin; last self-check showed 161M and Precision@1: 0.6178 on data/test.txt before starting another 50-epoch train on cleaned data.

failure_modes.name: Hyperparameter search non-convergence
failure_modes.description: After step 58 identified a 1.4GB model at 0.6115 accuracy, the agent ran seven more full supervised retrains over ~85 minutes. The best checkpoint (step 104) still missed both gates at 161M and Precision@1: 0.6178, then it began another epoch=50 training on cleaned data with under 18 minutes left before timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Precision@1: 0.6178

counterfactual.X: At step 104, shrink or quantize the 0.6178-accuracy model (e.g., fasttext quantize or train with very low dim like dim=5 per the reference solution) instead of launching another 50-epoch full retrain.
counterfactual.Y: After reaching 161M / 0.6178, the agent preprocessed text and started a new epoch=50 train_supervised on data/train_clean.txt while blocking on TaskOutput until the 7200s agent timeout.

failure_modes.name: Initial oversized training run
failure_modes.description: The first training used dim=100 and bucket=2000000, producing a 1.4GB /app/model.bin at 0.6115 accuracy. That configuration consumed roughly ten minutes and forced the agent into a long corrective search that never recovered enough wall-clock budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The model is 1.4GB (way over 150MB) and accuracy is 0.6115 (below 0.62).

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier scores a held-out private_test.txt via the fasttext CLI and enforces a byte-size cap on /app/model.bin; passing requires real model quality, not format tricks on public data.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 7200s harness timeout after repeated FastText retrains that never jointly met the 150MB size cap and 0.62 accuracy bar (best self-check: 161MB, P@1 0.6178).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test_outputs.py: `fasttext test /app/model.bin /tests/private_test.txt` must parse P@1 >= 0.62 (ACCURACY_THRESHOLD) and `os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin')` must be < 150MB (MAX_MODEL_SIZE).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Converted Yelp parquet to FastText `data/train.txt`/`data/test.txt`, installed fasttext via pip after many attempts, trained via Python `train_supervised` saving `/app/model.bin`; final completed checkpoint was 161MB with Precision@1 0.6178 on the agent's 10k-row `data/test.txt`, then started another background train on `train_clean.txt` at timeout.

failure_modes.name: Joint constraint non-convergence
failure_modes.description: After step 58 the agent recognized the 1.4GB/0.6115 model violated both limits, then ran seven more `train_supervised` jobs with shrinking dims/buckets but never achieved <150MB and >=0.62 together; the best run stayed 161MB at P@1 0.6178.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The model is 1.4GB (way over 150MB) and accuracy is 0.6115 (below 0.62). I need to reduce size significantly and improve accuracy.

counterfactual.X: Train with aggressively small FastText settings (e.g. `fasttext supervised` with `dim=5`, `wordNgrams=2` as in the reference solution) after parquet→train.txt conversion, then verify with `fasttext test /app/model.bin data/test.txt` for both size and P@1.
counterfactual.Y: Iteratively retuned Python `train_supervised` with dims 50→20, large buckets, and subword ngrams, producing models that remained >150MB and peaked at P@1 0.6178.

failure_modes.name: FastText install retry loop
failure_modes.description: Before any training, the agent spent steps 7–32 on eight pip install variants because fasttext wheels failed to build on the Python 3.13 image, consuming a large fraction of the 7200s budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
× Failed to build installable wheels for some pyproject.toml based projects
╰─> fasttext

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring uses a private `/tests/private_test.txt` tarball and checks real `fasttext test` accuracy plus on-disk model size; passing requires a genuinely small, accurate model, not a stdout stub.

outcome.headline: The agent timed out after seven full retrains on 650k samples, never delivering a model that simultaneously met the 150MB size cap and 0.62 accuracy requirement.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test_outputs.py asserts P@1 >= 0.62 from `fasttext test /app/model.bin /tests/private_test.txt` (lines 10-35) and os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin') < 150 * 1024 * 1024 (lines 38-41).
outcome.what_agent_produced: Saved /app/model.bin via Python fasttext.train_supervised; best checkpoint at step 105 was 161M (~169MB) with P@1 0.6178 on local data/test.txt; an eighth retrain on train_clean.txt was still running when the 7200s agent timeout hit at step 113.

failure_modes.name: Oversized first training run
failure_modes.description: Despite the 150MB constraint in the prompt, the agent's first training used dim=100 and bucket=2000000 on all 650k samples, producing a 1.4GB model at 0.6115 accuracy and forcing hours of corrective retrains.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: bucket=2000000

counterfactual.X: Start with aggressively small hyperparameters (e.g., dim=5–20, bucket≤50000, wordNgrams=2) matching the size-accuracy tradeoff required by the 150MB cap.
counterfactual.Y: Launched the first train with dim=100, epoch=5, bucket=2000000, yielding a 1.4GB /app/model.bin.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent retrain loop
failure_modes.description: After recognizing the size problem, the agent repeatedly retrained the full 650k-sample dataset with only incremental dim/bucket tweaks (50→25→20), never reaching both constraints, and was killed mid-wait on an eighth background training job.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 7200.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Misread size constraint
failure_modes.description: At step 91 the agent declared 161MB acceptable even though the task requires under 150MB, then continued tuning accuracy instead of compressing further.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Size is good at 161MB but accuracy is still below 0.62.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier evaluates a held-out private_test.txt with the fasttext CLI and enforces a hard byte-size ceiling; passing requires genuine model quality, not format tricks.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 7200s timeout while blocked on a final full-dataset retrain, after leaving /app/model.bin at 161MB and ~0.618 accuracy—missing both the 150MB and 0.62 gates.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: pytest in tests/test_outputs.py: fasttext test /app/model.bin on /tests/private_test.txt must yield P@1 >= 0.62 (lines 33-35) and os.path.getsize(/app/model.bin) < 150MB (lines 40-41)
outcome.what_agent_produced: Converted Yelp parquet to FastText format, installed fasttext for Python 3.13, trained via fasttext.train_supervised; initial /app/model.bin was 1.4GB at P@1 0.6115 on data/test.txt, then six+ full retrains shrinking size to 161–207MB with best public-test P@1 0.6178 before a dim=20 epoch=50 job on train_clean.txt was still running at timeout

failure_modes.name: Oversized initial hyperparameters
failure_modes.description: The first supervised train used dim=100 and bucket=2000000, producing a 1.4GB /app/model.bin at 0.6115 accuracy—already violating the 150MB cap and missing the 0.62 accuracy target before any refinement loop.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The model is 1.4GB (way over 150MB) and accuracy is 0.6115 (below 0.62).

counterfactual.X: Train a compact model early (e.g. fasttext supervised with -dim 5 -wordNgrams 2 as in task/solution/solve.sh) so /app/model.bin stays under 150MB while targeting >=0.62 accuracy
counterfactual.Y: Ran fasttext.train_supervised with dim=100, bucket=2000000, saving a 1.4GB model at Precision@1: 0.6115

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent retrain loop
failure_modes.description: After recognizing both constraint failures at step 58, the agent launched seven successive full-dataset Python retrains (steps 59–110) whose checkpoints still exceeded 150MB and never reached 0.62 on the public test split (best 0.6178 at 161MB); it was waiting on TaskOutput for another 50-epoch train when the harness raised AgentTimeoutError at 7200s.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Precision@1: 0.6178

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier scores a held-out /tests/private_test.txt archive, not the agent-accessible subsampled data/test parquet, so passing requires genuine generalization rather than overfitting the local 10k public split.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
